observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:
Instruct the Municipal Agriculture Office
To gather relevant records, with regards to the transfer of 10 heads of goats and 10 heads of cattle and submit copies of the same to the Municipal Accountant and GSO for basis of recording.
Initiate preparation of project policy or implementing guidelines for the conduct, sustainability, and monitoring of the program. Guidelines on the selection of beneficiaries should also be included in the policy.
Demand from the farmer the submission of the required documents related to 1 dead cattle and 7 dead goats, including justification from the Municipal Agriculture Office for not rendering said documents 30 days from death, to the Municipal Mayor’s Office, Municipal Accounting Office, and General Service Office for proper disposition and recording. Also, forward a complete copy of such documents to the Office of the Auditor.
In addition to the loan contract executed, initiate execution of MOA with the farmers who receives the 6 heads of heifers and 10 heads of goats stipulating all the necessary terms, conditions, and sanctions in case of non-performance of responsibilities.
For proper monitoring, prepare Program Monitoring Report at least quarterly containing, among others, the balances at the beginning of the quarter and the changes of the biological assets due to physical changes such as: a) growth, b) degeneration, c) production, and d) procreation.
Intensify monitoring of the livestocks, including monitoring of the offsprings of the animals in order to create more opportunities for other constituents of the LGU.
Abide the rules contained in the MOA between LGU Kalilangan and DA RFO 10, and MOA between the LGU and farmers.
Submit justification on the distribution of 10 heads of goats to 2 beneficiaries only and of the 6 heads of heifers to 2 beneficiaries only.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to
Prepare journal entry to the stocks once pertinent documents with regards to the grant are provided by the Municipal Agriculture Officer.
Ensure that Work, Other Animals, Breeding Stock Ledger Cards (WOABSLC) is prepared and maintained
Direct the GSO to:
Record the stocks once pertinent documents are provided by the Municipal Agriculture Officer.
Prepare Property Cards for all Breeding Stocks
Issue PAR or ICS to the recipient of the breeding stocks;
Ensure conduct of physical inventory at least once a year to monitor any the changes of the biological assets as to quantity and due to physical changes such as growth, degeneration, production, and procreation.

We recommended to the management to:
Direct the MSWDO and MHO to immediately submit the lacking documents, particularly Acknowledgement Receipts, together with the respective post-activity report for the implementation of the Nutrition and Feeding Program. And, prospectively, timely prepare and submit the mentioned documents to support the recording of expenses.
Immediately submit list of personnel assign and reporting in each quarter/detachment for issuance of supplies to AFP/PNP/BFP and CAFGU, lists of detainees for issuance of food supplies to Municipal Police Station, and list of construction workers during the construction period for the issuance of supplies to the engineering office. And, prospectively, timely prepare and submit the mentioned documents to support the recording of expenses.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents relevant to the recording of similar transactions and ensure recording of expenses related to food supplies for distribution is supported with RIS related to the issuances/distribution.
Direct GSO to have make another way of documenting the transfer of food supplies to MHO and MSWD for the conduct of nutrition and supplemental feeding program. And, direct the MHO and MSWD to facilitate the preparation of RIS, as well as the RSMI, upon actual issuance of supplies to the end-users. Also, timely submit RSMI and RIS and other pertinent documents to ensure timely recording of expenses.

We recommended to the management to:
Direct the GSO to henceforth:
Use the index-based pricing scheme for procurement of FOL in accordance with Section 61 of Republic Act No. 9184 (R.A. 9184), otherwise known as the “Government Procurement Reform Act” and its Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR), and the GPPB Guidelines for Contract Price Escalation.
Change the mode of delivery for procurement of FOL to staggered mode.
Ensure that the provisions of the entered contracts and POs are strictly followed, and process amendments to order as necessary;
Ensure that all submitted supporting documents are prepared to the highest standards of honesty, objectivity, and consistency, and do not depict any inaccurate or misleading information; and,
Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Ensure completeness of supporting documents relevant to the recorded transactions.
Draw an adjusting entry for the erroneous entry of recording fuel consumption of various offices amounting to P86,391.00.

We recommended to the Management to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to effect fund transfers for the remittance of the special accounts in the GF, SEF, and TF before the GF Proper would remit the mandatory deductions to the respective government agencies.

We recommended to the Management to instruct:
The Accounting Office to:
Trace the unidentified balance of Due to GSIS, HDMF, and PhilHealth accounts and prepare a schedule of unremitted contribution and loan payments.
To regularly reconcile the recorded premium contributions and loan payments with the monthly remittances and timely effect any adjusting entries to account for any variances.
Verify the noted unremitted premium contributions and loan payments and process the remittance thereof.
Ensure proper deduction and recording of premium contributions and loan payments and the timely remittance thereof.
The HRMO to:
timely update the GSIS portal to generate accurate data of the Statement of Accounts.
Ensure that JO personnel have HDMF account prior to the deduction of their voluntary contribution.

We recommended to the management to direct the HRMO and Municipal Accountant to ensure the attachment of the following to the disbursements of Salaries and PERA:
For SB members, attendance sheets to the SB's regular sessions, approved applications for leave, and other pertinent documents.
For regular and casual employees, DTRs and official business documents such as TO or NLS and CAs, as applicable.
Attach certification from the HRMO that absences, late arrivals, and undertimes were already posted/charged to their respective leave credits.
Moreover, submit the abovementioned lacking DTRs of employees and approved leave application forms of SB members, and charge any unrecorded absences to their respective leave credits.

We recommended to the Management to:
Strictly ensure that unit heads timely submit the PPMP and other pertinent documents in the prescribed format, accurate computations, and consistent data across related documents during the preparation and submission of budget proposals.
Strictly ensure that the consolidation of APP is done during the preparation and submission of budget proposals and that only revisions and finalization of documents will be made once the Appropriation Ordinance is approved.
Conduct orientation on budget preparation to avoid similar problems discussed in this audit finding.
To ensure that the timeline indicated in the APP is reasonable enough to ensure that the procured goods and services will be delivered timely as needed.

We recommended to the management to:
Submit immediately to the audit team the enumerated lacking documents to complete our post audit. Secure documents from the LMP and VMLP Bukidnon Chapter regarding the breakdown of paid registration fees to accurately account for the same, including the pertinent documents necessary for the proper liquidation thereof. Non-submission may warrant the issuance of an audit suspension or disallowance, as the case may be.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to, henceforth:
Ensure that payments for “registration fees” for purposes directly related to travel expenses should be treated as cash advances, subject to proper documentation and liquidation; and
Ensure that expenses related to foreign travel are recorded to the Travelling Expenses – Foreign account.
Ensure that every disbursement of government funds is safeguarded against illegal or improper disposition and that the funds are utilized efficiently, economically, and effectively.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive, being the primarily responsible official, to direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to timely submit the year-end financial statements and supporting schedules to allow ample time for review. Implement the necessary measures to meet, within the prescribed period, the preparation and submission of the required year-end financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to stop the improper practice of granting honoraria to the BAC Secretariat member performing regular full-time functions under the procurement unit. Instead, the management may consider granting overtime pay or per diem, if warranted.

Likewise, we recommended to the Municipal Accountant to incorporate withholding taxes on compensation income from honoraria payments to the payroll of BAC, TWG, and BAC Secretariat in order to avoid the adverse effect of non-withholding of taxes per Section 251 of RA No. 8424 “An Act Amending the National Internal Revenue Code”.
observations.managementAction: Honoraria for BAC Secretaria under the procurement unit was  removed from payroll for BAC honoraria. Withholding taxes were deducted from payroll for BAC honoraria.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned municipal officials to submit justification and pertinent documents, particularly RSMI and RSI in support to the JEV drawn, to fully account the distribution of medicines and other medical and laboratory supplies totaling P4.013 million. 

Likewise, we recommended to the Local Chief Executive to improve the agency’s property and supply management practices by requiring the following:

1)	The property custodian to: (a) Maintain Stock Cards to monitor the receipt and release of all procured supplies; (b) Require the duly filled-up Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) prior to release to the requestor as proof of extinguishing accountability over supplies; and (c) Prepare Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) to be submitted monthly to the Municipal Accountant supported with RIS.

2)	The Municipal Accountant to: (a) Maintain Supplies Ledger Cards to record the acquisition and issuance of supplies; (b) Require the property custodian to submit RSMI and RIS as basis for the dropping in the books of accounts; (c) Reconcile financial records with that of the property custodian as a check and balance measure.

3)	Require the General Services Officer (GSO) to focus on establishing a good property and supply management system and take initiative in the conduct of physical count of supplies and properties required at least annually.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to create an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of the property, plant and equipment to determine existence and physical condition of the assets. Direct the Inventory Committee and the concerned offices to exert extra efforts in reconciling the reports per physical inventory, per GSO and per accounting records. 

Require the GSO to establish good practices on supply and property management by extending efforts to update and maintain a complete record for supply and property accountabilities, such as Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR), Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) and property cards per category of PPE. 

Require the Disposal Committee to expedite the disposition of all unserviceable and obsolete properties for the municipality to avoid further deterioration and to generate additional income from their disposal. Specifically, the LCE should create Appraisal and Disposal Committees to undertake the appraisal and disposition of these properties in accordance to COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989 and PD 1445. Moreover, a request for audit action should be submitted to COA audit team before the conduct of proper disposal activity. The reports prepared should be furnished to the Municipal Accountant who shall prepare journal vouchers to drop the properties from the books of account.
observations.managementAction:
Issue Executive Order for creation of Inventory Committee.

Conduct physical Inventory of all PPE.

Maintain PAR and Property Cards.

Conduct disposal of unserviceable properties
observations.reason: Not given priority by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to revisit and review the proper account classification of the retentions withheld from suppliers, as prescribed by Revised Chart of Accounts for LGU under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015. Thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to reconcile the differences between the accounting records and the Consolidated Quarterly Report on Government Project/Activities to ensure that projects already completed should no longer be recorded under the CIP accounts. Moreover, implement an effective monitoring and evaluation control on the status of implementation of infrastructure and other projects by organizing a Local Project Monitoring Committee pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circulars No. 2004-78 and 2019-188 dated 22 June 2004 and 14 November 2019, respectively.
We further recommended to the Municipal Engineer to submit immediately to the Municipal Accountant the relevant documents such as Certificate of Completion and Inspection Report, and other pertinent documents as bases of the latter to reclassify the CIP account to its specific PPE account.
Furthermore, we recommended to the Municipal Accountant to conduct detailed analysis and effect necessary adjusting entries relative to the unreclassified accounts and the affected accumulated depreciations, if warranted.

We recommended to the management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the accrued interest expense incurred from the availed outstanding loans at year-end in observance to the accrual method of accounting interest expenses as prescribed by PPSAS and in order to improve the reliability and accuracy of the balances reflected in the financial statements. Further, reconcile the difference of P30,000.02 between the accounting and bank records and effect adjusting entry, if warranted, to enhance accuracy and reliability of outstanding loans payable reflected in the Statement of Financial Position. Furthermore, support loan amortization payments with billing statements to readily determine the validity and the reasonableness of the disbursed amounts.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entry to recognize the interest earned in CY 2019 in observance to the accrual method of accounting interest income as prescribed by PPSAS; and to the Municipal Treasurer to secure bank confirmation every reporting period to reconcile the balance reflected in the book over bank records to enhance its reliability and accuracy.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to record the remaining inventory of drugs and medicines at year-end as assets, particularly to the Drugs and Medicines Inventory account. Record the receipt and issuance of drugs and medicines using the perpetual inventory method, considering the proper accounts provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015. Further, we recommended to the Municipal Health Officer to furnish the Municipal Accountant the prescribed report on issued supplies as basis of the latter in the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the expended/issued drugs and medicines.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct and assure the speedy and periodic reconciliation of records maintained by the Accountant and Budget Officer at least every quarter in order to timely prepare and submit a Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) which is prescribed to be submitted annually, along with other financial reports, on or before 14th of February to the Commission on Audit.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to be prudent in authorizing the grant of cash advances to Cash Disbursing Officer.
We further recommended to the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to establish first the legal and specific purpose of the cash advance by detailing on its disbursement voucher and/or on its supporting document the identified payrolls and other claims to be paid out from the cash advance, in order to limit the disbursements thereof to its intended purpose/s.
Furthermore, evaluation of bottleneck procedures which ultimately causing delay in the processing of personnel payrolls and other claims must be given priority by management for a more effective and efficient local government financial operations.

We recommended to the management to strictly abide with the rules and regulations on the prescribed period of liquidation of specific cash advances as stated in Section 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. Likewise, we recommended to the Municipal Accountant to ensure that previous cash advances were first liquidated before granting additional cash advance to the accountable officer and to attach a certification in the cash advance voucher that previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned department heads to:
a) Submit justification as to why registration fees were imposed and paid through league/association/federation for LGU-conducted trainings, planning, assessment or other-related activities; 
b) Discontinue such improper practice, instead specifically detail in the activity/training design the budgetary requirements, such as payment for venue and/or hotel, supplies and honoraria of speakers and process the same by taking into consideration the applicable procurement laws and some other rules and regulations; and,
c) Stop the practice of attaching Certificates of Appearance issued by venue provider where the activities are conducted and secure this specific document from the identified focal person or any assigned official primarily accountable for the proper conduct and monitoring of such activity.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit within the prescribed period the required year-end FS and supporting schedules to the Auditor to give ample time for review and to avoid undue delay in rendering an audit opinion on the fairness of presentation of financial statements and the preparation of the Municipality’s CY 2018 Annual Audit Report. Moreover, implement adequate measures that will enable the agency to meet such deadline.

We recommended to require the Municipal Treasurer and Liquidating Officer to diligently monitor the remittance of cash collections received by the collecting officers. Include in the CRAAF check accountabilities and maintain logbook for checks received/purchased. Thoroughly review the RAAF and CRAAF if the data reflected are correct and without errors before submission to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to direct the Municipal Accountant and other accountable officers to submit within the prescribed deadlines the required accounting and financial reports to the Auditor to give ample time for review. Further, implement adequate measures that will enable the agency to meet such deadlines.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort in the reconciliation of cash book balances with the accounting records. Communicate with the Municipal Accountant to trace up reconciling items in the current and prior periods to make the cash books accurate and reliable. Further, reconcile with the Accounting, as frequent as possible, to ensure prompt detection of discrepancies, the parties at fault, and application of proper action/remedy to correct them.

We recommended Management to demand immediate settlement of the said special cash advances granted to special disbursing officers; non-observance thereof will cause the withholding of the accountable officers’ respective salaries. Compliance to the rules and regulations in the timely liquidation of cash advances should be strictly enjoined.

We recommended Management to require the Municipal Assessor to register all land properties in favor of the municipality under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 to avoid possible land disputes in the future; instruct Accounting personnel handling the account to ensure that all recorded transactions have been adequately supported for a fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements; require the GSO to maintain complete records and documents of all the properties of the municipality under his charge pursuant to Sections 375 & 490 of RA 7160; and, require the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate funds needed to secure Torrens Certificate of Title for the LGU’s real estates.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should responsibly furnish the COA audit team complete copies of documents as relevant elements of perfected contracts for auditorial review and technical evaluation thereof. Further, we remind the BAC to carefully examine these documents before submission to the auditor so as not to contribute to the delay in the review of contract as explicitly provided in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the General Service Office (GSO) be instructed to submit copy of notice of deliveries to the Office of the Auditor on purchases made for review and inspection to attain timely and relevant audit results and to facilitate post audit of transaction.  This notification requirement was embodied in COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommended Management to prepare PPMP for all programs and/or projects including those funded by the 20% LDF.  The preparation of PPMPs is consistent with the requirement of the procurement law and that of the Government Procurement Policy Board-Technical Support Office (GPPB-TSO) which should be observed and implemented by all government agencies.  Unless PPMPs are formulated, actual procurement may result to either non-implementation or delay, in the absence of the pertinent procurement schedules and inexact terms of technical specifications, scope of work, or terms of reference specifically needed by the end-user or implementing units.

It is recommended that Management should closely observe the provisions provided in the aforementioned regulations with            regards to maintaining depreciation expense regularly.  We recommended further that Management shall continue with its action on retrieving the pertinent supporting records/ documents that contain facts like acquisition date which is one essential factor in order to properly establish the correct expense amount for the period.

It is recommended that Management should take appropriate actions, particularly in deciding to officially transfer the said constructed school buildings and other facilities to Department of Education and prepare such necessary turn-over documents.

It is recommended that Management should require the Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare on a monthly basis the Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF using the prescribed format in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and, submit such report on or before the 15th day after the end of the month through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council to the COA Auditor in compliance with the reportorial requirements of the aforecited COA Circular.
